title: Midterm Examination — English Grammar (M.1)
description: Covers singular & plural nouns, demonstratives, imperatives, present simple vs. present continuous, and comparative & superlative adjectives.

sections.title: Grammar Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the best answer to complete each sentence.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: I have three ______ at home.
questions.explanation: 'Child' is an irregular plural noun: children.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: ______ are my new shoes over there.
questions.explanation: 'Shoes' is plural and far away → the demonstrative 'Those'.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: A car is ______ than a bicycle.
questions.explanation: Short adjective + -er forms the comparative: faster than.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: This is the ______ book in the library.
questions.explanation: Longer adjectives use 'the most' for the superlative.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Every day she ______ to school with her friends.
questions.explanation: 'Every day' is a routine → present simple, third person singular: walks.

sections.title: Grammar in Context
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the paragraph and choose the correct word for each blank.

passages.title: A Sunday Afternoon
passages.passage: My family lives in Chiang Mai. Every Sunday, we ___(6)___ to the park together. Right now, my father ___(7)___ a book under a tree, and my little sister ___(8)___ with the dog.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Blank (6): Every Sunday, we ______ to the park.
questions.explanation: 'Every Sunday' signals a routine → present simple with 'we': go.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Blank (7): Right now, my father ______ a book.
questions.explanation: 'Right now' → present continuous: is reading.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Blank (8): My little sister ______ with the dog.
questions.explanation: Action happening at this moment → present continuous: is playing.

sections.title: Error Identification
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the underlined part that is grammatically incorrect.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: This exercise is (A) more (B) easier (C) than (D) the last one.
questions.explanation: Do not use 'more' with an -er comparative. Say 'easier than', not 'more easier than'.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: Look at (A) those (B) woman (C) over (D) there.
questions.explanation: 'Those' is plural, so the noun must be plural: women.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: She (A) is (B) go (C) to school (D) now.
questions.explanation: With 'now' and 'is', use the -ing form: going.

sections.title: Reading Comprehension
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow. Questions 12–15 refer to this passage.

passages.title: A Day in Bangkok
passages.passage: Bangkok is one of the largest cities in Thailand. Its population is bigger than the population of any other city in the country. Right now, many tourists are visiting the city. They are taking photos of the Grand Palace and walking along the Chao Phraya River. There are also many street markets. These markets sell fresh fruit, hot food, and small gifts. The most popular market is Chatuchak, which opens on weekends. It is the biggest weekend market in Asia.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: According to the passage, Chatuchak is the ______ weekend market in Asia.
questions.explanation: The passage says 'It is the biggest weekend market in Asia' — superlative form.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: What are the tourists doing at the Grand Palace right now?
questions.explanation: 'They are taking photos of the Grand Palace' — present continuous.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: Which of the following from the passage is a plural noun?
questions.explanation: 'Tourists' ends in -s and refers to more than one — a plural noun.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: The passage says Bangkok's population is ______ than the population of any other city.
questions.explanation: The comparative 'bigger than' is used to compare two things.

sections.title: Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the word or sentence that best answers each question.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: The plural of 'mouse' is ______ .
questions.explanation: 'Mouse' has an irregular plural form: mice.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: The comparative form of 'good' is ______ .
questions.explanation: 'Good' is irregular: good → better → best.

questions.topic: Unit 4: Comparative & Superlative Adjectives
questions.question: The superlative form of 'bad' is ______ .
questions.explanation: 'Bad' is irregular: bad → worse → worst.

questions.topic: Introduction 0.2: Singular & Plural Nouns, Demonstratives, and Imperatives
questions.question: Which sentence is an imperative?
questions.explanation: An imperative gives a command and starts with the base form of the verb.

questions.topic: Unit 3: Present Continuous Tense, Present Simple vs. Present Continuous Tense
questions.question: Which sentence is in the present continuous tense?
choices: I read a book every night.
I am reading a book now.
I will read a book tomorrow.
questions.answer: I am reading a book now.
questions.explanation: Present continuous = am/is/are + verb-ing, describing an action happening now.
